data.text: I'll start by reading the key files mentioned in the bead description to understand the current codebase structure.
Now let me look at existing test files for install and update commands:

data.output:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"strings"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)

15	// makeLocalPlugin creates a minimal plugin fixture at dir with the given name
16	// and one skill. It returns the skill directory path.
17	func makeLocalPlugin(t *testing.T, dir, name string) string {
18		t.Helper()
19		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(
20			"name: "+name+"\n"+
21				"version: 1.0.0\n"+
22				"description: Test plugin\n"+
23				"type: plugin\n"+
24				"source: https://example.com/"+name+"\n"+
25				"api_version: \"1\"\n"+
26				"install:\n"+
27				"  root:\n"+
28				"    source: .\n"+
29				"    target: .ddx/plugins/"+name+"\n"+
30				"  skills:\n"+
31				"    - source: skills/\n"+
32				"      target: .agents/skills/\n"+
33				"    - source: skills/\n"+
34				"      target: .claude/skills/\n",
35		), 0o644))
36	
37		skillDir := filepath.Join(dir, "skills", name+"-skill")
38		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
39		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte(
40			"---\nname: "+name+"-skill\ndescription: Test skill\n---\n\nBody.\n",
41		), 0o644))
42		return skillDir
43	}
44	
45	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks verifies AC1:
46	// ddx install <name> --global writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
47	// and creates agent-tier links under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
48	func TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks(t *testing.T) {
49		workDir := t.TempDir()
50		homeDir := t.TempDir()
51		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
52		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
53		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
54	
55		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
56		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
57	
58		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
59		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--global", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
60		require.NoError(t, err, output)
61	
62		// Plugin root must land in the global tree.
63		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "myplugin")
64		info, statErr := os.Lstat(globalPluginDir)
65		require.NoError(t, statErr, "global plugin dir must exist")
66		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "global plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
67	
68		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory.
69		// Local installs create symlinks, so use os.Lstat (DirExists follows symlinks).
70		agentSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
71		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
72		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
73		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "~/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
74		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
75			"~/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
76		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
77		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "~/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
78		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
79			"~/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
80	
81		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
82		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
83		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
84		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
85		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")
86	}
87	
88	// TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks verifies AC2:
89	// ddx install <name> (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/
90	// and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and
91	// <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
92	func TestInstallProject_InTreeLinks(t *testing.T) {
93		workDir := t.TempDir()
94		homeDir := t.TempDir()
95		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
96	
97		// Force in-tree mode by pre-creating .ddx/.
98		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
99	
100		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
101		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
102	
103		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
104		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
105		require.NoError(t, err, output)
106	
107		// Plugin must land in the project in-tree location.
108		projectPluginDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "myplugin")
109		info, statErr := os.Lstat(projectPluginDir)
110		require.NoError(t, statErr, "project plugin dir must exist")
111		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "project plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
112	
113		// Project-tier skill links must be in the project directory.
114		// Local installs create symlinks, so use os.Lstat.
115		agentSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
116		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
117		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
118		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for in-tree install")
119		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
120			"<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for in-tree install")
121		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
122		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for in-tree install")
123		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
124			"<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for in-tree install")
125	
126		// Nothing must land in the home directory's skill dirs.
127		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
128		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "home .agents/skills must not be created for in-tree install")
129		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
130		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "home .claude/skills must not be created for in-tree install")
131	}
132	
133	// TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks verifies AC3:
134	// ddx install <name> (no --global, convention mode — no .ddx/ in project) installs
135	// to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/ and creates
136	// project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.
137	func TestInstallProject_ConventionLinks(t *testing.T) {
138		workDir := t.TempDir()
139		homeDir := t.TempDir()
140		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
141		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
142		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
143	
144		// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
145	
146		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
147		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
148	
149		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
150		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
151		require.NoError(t, err, output)
152	
153		// Determine the convention root that ddxroot.Path would produce.
154		conventionRoot := ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), workDir)
155	
156		// Plugin must land under the XDG convention root.
157		conventionPluginDir := filepath.Join(conventionRoot, "plugins", "myplugin")
158		info, statErr := os.Lstat(conventionPluginDir)
159		require.NoError(t, statErr, "convention plugin dir must exist at %s", conventionPluginDir)
160		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "convention plugin dir must be a symlink")
161	
162		// The convention root must NOT be the in-tree path (no .ddx/ was created in workDir).
163		assert.False(t, conventionRoot == filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName),
164			"convention root must differ from in-tree path when .ddx/ was not pre-created")
165	
166		// Project-tier skill links must be in the project directory (not in convention root).
167		// Local installs create symlinks, so use os.Lstat.
168		agentSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
169		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
170		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
171		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for convention install")
172		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
173			"<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for convention install")
174		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
175		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for convention install")
176		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
177			"<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for convention install")
178	}
179	
180	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndLinksAgentTier verifies bead AC1:
181	// ddx install <name> --global --silent writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
182	// and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>
183	// that resolves into the global plugin tree.
184	func TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndLinksAgentTier(t *testing.T) {
185		workDir := t.TempDir()
186		homeDir := t.TempDir()
187		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
188		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
189		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
190	
191		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
192		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
193	
194		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
195		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--global", "--silent", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
196		require.NoError(t, err, output)
197	
198		// Plugin root must land in the global tree.
199		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "myplugin")
200		info, statErr := os.Lstat(globalPluginDir)
201		require.NoError(t, statErr, "global plugin dir must exist at %s", globalPluginDir)
202		assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || info.IsDir(),
203			"global plugin dir must be a symlink or dir")
204	
205		// Resolve the global plugin dir to its canonical path so we can verify
206		// that skill links resolve into the same physical tree.
207		realGlobalPlugin, evalErr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(globalPluginDir)
208		require.NoError(t, evalErr, "global plugin dir must be resolvable")
209	
210		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory and resolve into
211		// the global plugin tree.
212		for _, surface := range []string{".agents/skills", ".claude/skills"} {
213			skillLink := filepath.Join(homeDir, surface, "myplugin-skill")
214			skillInfo, skillErr := os.Lstat(skillLink)
215			require.NoError(t, skillErr, "%s must exist for global install", skillLink)
216			assert.True(t, skillInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || skillInfo.IsDir(),
217				"%s must be a symlink or dir for global install", skillLink)
218	
219			// Verify the skill resolves into the global plugin tree.
220			realSkill, skillEvalErr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(skillLink)
221			require.NoError(t, skillEvalErr, "%s must be resolvable", skillLink)
222			assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(realSkill, realGlobalPlugin),
223				"%s must resolve into global plugin tree %s; got %s", skillLink, realGlobalPlugin, realSkill)
224		}
225	
226		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
227		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
228		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
229		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
230		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")
231	}
232	
233	// TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTierAndAgentLinks verifies AC1:
234	// ddx install <name> --global --silent writes to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/
235	// and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.

243		localPlugin := t.TempDir()

246		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)

256	
257		// Agent-tier skill links must be in the home directory.
258		agentSkill := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")

260		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
261		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "~/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for global install")
262		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
263			"~/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
264		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)

266		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
267			"~/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for global install")
268	
269		// Nothing must land in the project directory's skill dirs.
270		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
271		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "project .agents/skills must not be created for global install")
272		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
273		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "project .claude/skills must not be created for global install")

276	// TestInstallProject_InTreeMode verifies AC2:
277	// ddx install <name> (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/
278	// and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and
279	// <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>.
280	func TestInstallProject_InTreeMode(t *testing.T) {
281		workDir := t.TempDir()
282		homeDir := t.TempDir()
283		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
284	
285		// Force in-tree mode by pre-creating .ddx/.
286		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
287	
288		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
289		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
290	
291		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
292		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
293		require.NoError(t, err, output)
294	
295		// Plugin must land in the project in-tree location.
296		projectPluginDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "myplugin")
297		pluginInfo, pluginStatErr := os.Lstat(projectPluginDir)
298		require.NoError(t, pluginStatErr, "project plugin dir must exist at %s", projectPluginDir)
299		assert.True(t, pluginInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "project plugin dir must be a symlink")
300	
301		// Project-tier skill links must be in the project directory and point into the plugin dir.
302		agentSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
303		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
304		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
305		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for in-tree install")
306		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
307			"<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for in-tree install")
308		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
309		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for in-tree install")
310		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
311			"<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for in-tree install")
312	
313		// Skill links must resolve into the in-tree plugin directory.
314		agentTarget, agentReadErr := os.Readlink(agentSkill)
315		require.NoError(t, agentReadErr, "agent skill must be a symlink")
316		if !filepath.IsAbs(agentTarget) {
317			agentTarget = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(agentSkill), agentTarget)
318		}
319		agentTarget, _ = filepath.Abs(agentTarget)
320		assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(agentTarget, localPlugin),
321			"agent skill symlink must resolve into the local plugin dir; got %s", agentTarget)
322	
323		// Nothing must land in the home directory's skill dirs.
324		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
325		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "home .agents/skills must not be created for in-tree install")
326	}
327	
328	// TestInstallProject_ConventionMode verifies AC3:
329	// ddx install <name> (no --global, convention mode — no .ddx/ in project) installs
330	// to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/ and creates
331	// project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.
332	func TestInstallProject_ConventionMode(t *testing.T) {
333		workDir := t.TempDir()
334		homeDir := t.TempDir()
335		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
336		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
337		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
338	
339		// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
340		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
341		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
342	
343		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
344		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
345		require.NoError(t, err, output)
346	
347		// Determine the convention root that ddxroot.Path would produce.
348		conventionRoot := ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), workDir)
349	
350		// Plugin must land under the XDG convention root.
351		conventionPluginDir := filepath.Join(conventionRoot, "plugins", "myplugin")
352		pluginInfo, pluginStatErr := os.Lstat(conventionPluginDir)
353		require.NoError(t, pluginStatErr, "convention plugin dir must exist at %s", conventionPluginDir)
354		assert.True(t, pluginInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "convention plugin dir must be a symlink")
355	
356		// The convention root must be XDG-based, not in-tree.
357		assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(conventionRoot, xdgDataHome),
358			"convention root must be under XDG_DATA_HOME; got %s", conventionRoot)
359	
360		// Project-tier skill links must be in the project directory.
361		agentSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
362		claudeSkill := filepath.Join(workDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill")
363		agentInfo, agentErr := os.Lstat(agentSkill)
364		require.NoError(t, agentErr, "<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must exist for convention install")
365		assert.True(t, agentInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || agentInfo.IsDir(),
366			"<project>/.agents/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for convention install")
367		claudeInfo, claudeErr := os.Lstat(claudeSkill)
368		require.NoError(t, claudeErr, "<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must exist for convention install")
369		assert.True(t, claudeInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || claudeInfo.IsDir(),
370			"<project>/.claude/skills/<name> must be a symlink or dir for convention install")
371	
372		// Skill links must resolve into the local plugin source.
373		agentTarget, agentReadErr := os.Readlink(agentSkill)
374		require.NoError(t, agentReadErr, "agent skill must be a symlink")
375		if !filepath.IsAbs(agentTarget) {
376			agentTarget = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(agentSkill), agentTarget)
377		}
378		agentTarget, _ = filepath.Abs(agentTarget)
379		assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(agentTarget, localPlugin),
380			"agent skill symlink must resolve into the local plugin dir; got %s", agentTarget)
381	
382		// Home directory must not be polluted.
383		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
384		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "home .agents/skills must not be created for convention install")
385	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"os/exec"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
10		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/registry"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// commitTestFiles stages and commits the listed project-relative paths.
17	func commitTestFiles(t *testing.T, dir string, relPaths ...string) {
18		t.Helper()
19		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
20		for _, rel := range relPaths {
21			add := exec.Command("git", "add", rel)
22			add.Dir = dir
23			add.Env = cleanEnv
24			require.NoError(t, add.Run(), "git add %s", rel)
25		}
26		commit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "test: commit update target files")
27		commit.Dir = dir
28		commit.Env = cleanEnv
29		require.NoError(t, commit.Run(), "git commit")
30	}
31	
32	// writeRegistryInstalled writes a fake installed.yaml under ~HOME/.ddx/ so that
33	// performUpdate sees the listed files as registry-installed update targets.
34	func writeRegistryInstalled(t *testing.T, entries []registry.InstalledEntry) {
35		t.Helper()
36		state := &registry.InstalledState{Installed: entries}
37		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
38	}
39	
40	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
41	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
42	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
43	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
44	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
45		homeDir := t.TempDir()
46		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
47	
48		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
49	
50		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
51		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
52		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
53	
54		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
55		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
56		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
57		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
58	
59		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
60		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
63	
64		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
65		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
66		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
67		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
68		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
69		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
70		_ = err
71	
72		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
73		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
74		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
76			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
77			return
78		}
79		require.NoError(t, err)
80		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
81			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
82		}
83	}
84	
85	func TestUpdate_DoesNotAttemptBinaryUpgrade(t *testing.T) {
86		homeDir := t.TempDir()
87		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
88	
89		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
90	
91		output, err := te.RunCommand("update")
92		require.NoError(t, err)
93	
94		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Checking for DDx updates")
95		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Upgrading DDx")
96		assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
97	}

99	// TestUpdate_RefusesDirtyLibraryFile asserts ddx update --force exits non-zero
100	// when a registry-installed file (library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md) has uncommitted
101	// changes and the new content would differ. The error output must mention
102	// --force --discard-local [AC1].
103	func TestUpdate_RefusesDirtyLibraryFile(t *testing.T) {
104		homeDir := t.TempDir()
105		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
106	
107		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
108	
109		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
110		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
111			Name:    "ddx",
112			Version: "0.4.7",
113			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
114			Files:   []string{installedFile},
115		}})
116	
117		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original content\n")
118		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
119	
120		// Modify without staging → dirty worktree
121		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "modified content — not yet committed\n")
122	
123		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
124	
125		require.Error(t, err, "update --force should fail when an update-target file is dirty")
126		assert.Contains(t, output, "--force --discard-local",
127			"error output should mention --force --discard-local; got: %q", output)
128	}
129	
130	// TestUpdate_AllowsCleanLibraryFile asserts ddx update --force proceeds without
131	// a dirty-file error when the registry-installed file has no uncommitted changes
132	// [AC2].
133	func TestUpdate_AllowsCleanLibraryFile(t *testing.T) {
134		homeDir := t.TempDir()
135		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
136	
137		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
138	
139		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
140		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
141			Name:    "ddx",
142			Version: "0.4.7",
143			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
144			Files:   []string{installedFile},
145		}})
146	
147		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original content\n")
148		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
149	
150		// File is clean — no modification after commit.
151	
152		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
153	
154		// A network error from InstallPackage is tolerated; the test only asserts
155		// that the dirty-file guard did NOT fire.
156		_ = err
157		assert.NotContains(t, output, "--force --discard-local",
158			"clean file should not trigger the dirty-file error; got: %q", output)
159	}
160	
161	// TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty asserts that ddx update --force
162	// --discard-local succeeds when an installed file is dirty, and that the
163	// original content is saved to .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/<path> [AC3].
164	func TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty(t *testing.T) {
165		homeDir := t.TempDir()
166		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
167	
168		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
169	
170		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
171		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
172			Name:    "ddx",
173			Version: "0.4.7",
174			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
175			Files:   []string{installedFile},
176		}})
177	
178		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original committed content\n")
179		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
180	
181		// Make dirty — this is the content that must be saved in the backup.
182		dirtyContent := "modified content — not yet committed\n"
183		te.CreateFile(installedFile, dirtyContent)
184	
185		// Run with --discard-local; tolerate network errors from InstallPackage.
186		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force", "--discard-local")
187		_ = err
188	
189		// Must NOT produce the dirty-file error.
190		assert.NotContains(t, output, "--force --discard-local", /*as error msg*/
191			"--discard-local should suppress the dirty-file block; got: %q", output)
192	
193		// Backup directory must exist under .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/
194		backupRoot := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName, "update-backup")
195		entries, readErr := os.ReadDir(backupRoot)
196		require.NoError(t, readErr, "update-backup directory should be created")
197		require.NotEmpty(t, entries, "at least one timestamped backup dir should exist")
198	
199		// The backup file must contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content so the
200		// operator can recover their uncommitted changes.
201		backupFile := filepath.Join(backupRoot, entries[0].Name(), installedFile)
202		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(backupFile)
203		require.NoError(t, readErr, "backup file should be readable: %s", backupFile)
204		assert.Equal(t, dirtyContent, string(data),
205			"backup should contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content")
206	}
207	
208	// TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject verifies AC1:
209	// ddx update --global modifies only ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ and
210	// leaves the project plugins/ dir byte-identical.
211	func TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject(t *testing.T) {
212		workDir := t.TempDir()
213		homeDir := t.TempDir()
214		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
215		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
216		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
217	
218		// Seed a sentinel in the project plugin tree to verify it is not modified.
219		projectPluginDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "helix")
220		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectPluginDir, 0o755))
221		projectSentinel := filepath.Join(projectPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
222		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(projectSentinel, []byte("project-side"), 0o644))
223	
224		// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree.
225		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
226		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
227		globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
228		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
229	
230		// Run ddx update --global with empty global installed state (no packages to update).
231		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
232		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update", "--global")
233		require.NoError(t, err, output)
234	
235		// Project plugin tree must be byte-identical after global update.
236		projectData, readErr := os.ReadFile(projectSentinel)
237		require.NoError(t, readErr, "project sentinel must exist after global update")
238		assert.Equal(t, "project-side", string(projectData), "project sentinel must be byte-identical")
239	
240		// Global update must not trigger project-level skill refresh.
241		assert.NotContains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
242	}
243	
244	// TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode verifies AC2:
245	// ddx update (no --global) updates the project install in the active mode and
246	// never touches the global plugin tree.
247	func TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode(t *testing.T) {
248		t.Run("in-tree", func(t *testing.T) {
249			workDir := t.TempDir()
250			homeDir := t.TempDir()
251			xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
252			t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
253			t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
254	
255			// Force in-tree mode.
256			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
257	
258			// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree to verify it is not touched.
259			globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
260			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
261			globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
262			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
263	
264			writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{})
265	
266			factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
267			output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update")
268			require.NoError(t, err, output)
269	
270			// Shipped skills should be refreshed in the in-tree project.
271			assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
272	
273			// Global tree must be byte-identical after project update.
274			globalData, readErr := os.ReadFile(globalSentinel)
275			require.NoError(t, readErr, "global sentinel must exist after in-tree project update")
276			assert.Equal(t, "global-side", string(globalData), "global sentinel must be byte-identical")
277		})
278	
279		t.Run("convention", func(t *testing.T) {
280			workDir := t.TempDir()
281			homeDir := t.TempDir()
282			xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
283			t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
284			t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
285	
286			// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
287	
288			// Seed a sentinel in the global plugin tree.
289			globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "helix")
290			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(globalPluginDir, 0o755))
291			globalSentinel := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "sentinel.txt")
292			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(globalSentinel, []byte("global-side"), 0o644))
293	
294			writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{})
295	
296			factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
297			output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "update")
298			require.NoError(t, err, output)
299	
300			// Shipped skills should be refreshed in the project dir.
301			assert.Contains(t, output, "Shipped skills refreshed")
302	
303			// Global tree must be byte-identical after convention-mode project update.
304			globalData, readErr := os.ReadFile(globalSentinel)
305			require.NoError(t, readErr, "global sentinel must exist after convention project update")
306			assert.Equal(t, "global-side", string(globalData), "global sentinel must be byte-identical")
307		})
308	}
309	
310	// TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly asserts that when multiple
311	// registry-installed files are slated for overwrite and even ONE is dirty, the
312	// entire update halts before any file is mutated — clean files remain untouched
313	// [AC4].
314	func TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly(t *testing.T) {
315		homeDir := t.TempDir()
316		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
317	
318		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
319	
320		dirtyFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
321		cleanFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "reference", "work.md")
322		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
323			Name:    "ddx",
324			Version: "0.4.7",
325			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
326			Files:   []string{dirtyFile, cleanFile},
327		}})
328	
329		te.CreateFile(dirtyFile, "dirty file content\n")
330		te.CreateFile(cleanFile, "clean file content\n")
331		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, dirtyFile, cleanFile)
332	
333		// Only modify the dirty file
334		te.CreateFile(dirtyFile, "modified and uncommitted\n")
335	
336		_, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
337		require.Error(t, err, "update should fail when any target file is dirty")
338	
339		// The clean file must NOT have been mutated by the aborted update.
340		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(te.Dir, cleanFile))
341		require.NoError(t, readErr)
342		assert.Equal(t, "clean file content\n", string(data),
343			"clean file must be unchanged after atomic refuse")
344	
345		// refreshShippedSkills must NOT have run — .agents/skills/ddx/ should
346		// not have been created.
347		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills", "ddx"))
348		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
349			".agents/skills/ddx should not exist after atomic refuse")
350	}
